perturbation.original_text: 5.1 FEES AND EXPENSES Client will pay fees to Developer for Project Services and Deliverables as Described in the SOW (the Fees). Developer will pay its own expenses for the Project (collectively, the Expenses) unless stated otherwise in the SOW.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 FEES AND EXPENSES Client will pay fees to Developer for Project Services and Deliverables as Described in the SOW (the Fees). Client will also be responsible for all of the Developer's expenses for the Project (collectively, the Expenses) unless stated otherwise in the SOW.
perturbation.explanation: The original section states that Developer pays its own expenses. The modified version states that Client pays Developer's expenses, creating a direct contradiction. This will lead to disputes over who is responsible for expenses.The contradictory changes are being made within the section 5.1 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 FEES AND EXPENSES Client will pay fees to Developer for Project Services and Deliverables as Described in the SOW (the Fees). Client will also be responsible for all of the Developer's expenses for the Project (collectively, the Expenses) unless stated otherwise in the SOW.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Developer pays its own expenses, while the changed text states the Client is responsible for the Developer's expenses. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding financial responsibility for project expenses within section 5.1, as both versions of the same section are claiming opposite responsibilities. As such, this constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.5 ASSIGNMENT This Agreement will be binding on and inure to the benefit of the Parties their respective successors, assigns, heirs and personal representatives. Unless as provided herein, neither Party may assign, delegate, assign, nor subcontract their obligations and duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the non-assigning Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Developer may assign it rights and benefits under this Agreement, and delegate the performance of its obligations and duties hereunder, to any corporation or unincorporated business that is the successor to the business of Developer, without Client express or implied authorization.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.5 ASSIGNMENT This Agreement will be binding on and inure to the benefit of the Parties their respective successors, assigns, heirs and personal representatives. Unless as provided herein, either Party may assign, delegate, assign, nor subcontract their obligations and duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the non-assigning Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Developer may assign it rights and benefits under this Agreement, and delegate the performance of its obligations and duties hereunder, to any corporation or unincorporated business that is the successor to the business of Developer, without Client express or implied authorization.
perturbation.explanation: The original assignment clause states that 'neither Party may assign, delegate, assign, nor subcontract their obligations and duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the non-assigning Party.' The modified version states that 'either Party may assign, delegate, assign, nor subcontract their obligations and duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the non-assigning Party.'This contradicts the original clause and creates uncertainty about the assignment rights of both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.5 ASSIGNMENT This Agreement will be binding on and inure to the benefit of the Parties their respective successors, assigns, heirs and personal representatives. Unless as provided herein, either Party may assign, delegate, assign, nor subcontract their obligations and duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the non-assigning Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Developer may assign it rights and benefits under this Agreement, and delegate the performance of its obligations and duties hereunder, to any corporation or unincorporated business that is the successor to the business of Developer, without Client express or implied authorization.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'neither Party may assign... without the prior written consent...', whereas the changed text states that 'either Party may assign... without the prior written consent...'. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction, as the original version restricts both parties unless consent is given, and the modified version grants assignment rights to both parties unless consent is given, and the contradiction is triggered in the contradicted section because the contradiction is happening within the section itself, causing uncertainty and potential legal disputes as to who has assignment rights.
